query: Perplexity Computer Brain persistent memory system research preview launch date

{"query":"Perplexity Computer Brain persistent memory system research preview launch date","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1594,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.perplexity.ai/hub/blog/self-improving-memory-for-agents","title":"Self-improving Memory for Agents","content":"Today we are launching a wholly new approach to memory in AI.
Brain is a self-improving memory system.
Every day, Computer with Brain has a better starting point for each new task.
Agents become more effective at updating context as they learn the projects, connectors, artifacts, and other sources that lead to the best outputs.
The context layer takes the form of an LLM wiki that’s automatically loaded onto the agent sandbox.
Brain also shows its work, like other outputs generated by Perplexity.
Agents learn and remember the work and how to get better at it.
When agents actively learn from the work we do, they can identify opportunities we didn’t ask about, or flag a problem before anyone has noticed.
Brain is rolling out today to Max and Enterprise Max subscribers in Research Preview."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/perplexity-ai_introducing-brain-in-computer-brain-is-activity-7473407906034454528-j-0i","title":"Brain in Computer Improves Task Accuracy and Efficiency | Perplexity posted on the topic | LinkedIn","content":"Brain is a continuously learning memory system.
Available as a research preview for all Perplexity Max subscribers.
Perplexity shipped Brain in Computer this week.
We don't start every decision from scratch.
The interesting research question isn't just whether AI can remember.
Most AI tools today behave like they have amnesia.
If you pipe that into a persistent context graph, you don’t get a smarter AI.
Another clear sign that persistent knowledge bases, with continuous learning is what will ultimately differentiate your AI usage from the others."},{"url":"https://tech.yahoo.com/ai/perplexity-ai/articles/perplexitys-ai-agent-now-brain-194721920.html","title":"Perplexity's AI Agent Now Has a Brain That Learns From Its Own Mistakes","content":"Perplexity launched Brain today, a memory system for its Computer agent that gets smarter the more you use it.
"With Brain, Computer starts each task with full context of your projects, decisions, and sources instead of from scratch," Perplexity says.
Perplexity's own early metrics show Brain boosts answer correctness by 25% on tasks Computer has already handled, improves recall by 16%, and cuts the cost of context-heavy tasks by 13%.
But the direction makes sense: An agent that starts each morning knowing which sources failed last week will waste fewer tokens finding that out again.
OpenClaw—which has accumulated over 379,000 GitHub stars since launch—has been doing versions of this for months, using markdown files and a SQLite database with FTS5 full-text search to persist context across sessions.
With the Mem0 plugin, memory capture happens automatically at the system layer and survives restarts and context compaction.
It also has skills with similar propositions (like Obsidian Mind) that aim at making the agent more personal and more useful.
Both tools are self-hosted.
Perplexity's infrastructure holds the context graph, the LLM wiki, and all session history.
It's also worth being clear about what "self-improving" does and doesn't mean here.
Perplexity says new capabilities are coming soon, with no timeline attached."},{"url":"https://www.perplexity.ai/changelog/brain-faster-computer-models-website-publishing","title":"Perplexity Changelog","date":"4 days ago","content":"4 days ago — July 13, 2026. Brain: self-improving memory in Computer Computer now learns from every task. Brain builds a private context graph across your ..."}]}

{"query":"Perplexity Computer Brain persistent memory system research preview launch date","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.cnbctv18.com/technology/perplexity-launches-ai-computer-to-research-code-and-manage-projects-end-to-end-ws-l-19858309.htm/amp","title":"Perplexity launches AI 'Computer' to research, code and manage projects ...","content":"February 26, 2026, on Wednesday, February 25, February 25, 2026 “Files, tools, memory, and models, The system includes persistent memory,","score":0.81665546,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://explainx.ai/blog/perplexity-brain-computer-memory-system-2026","title":"Perplexity Brain: Self-Improving AI Memory for Computer Agent (2026) | explainx.ai Blog | explainx.ai","content":"# Perplexity Brain: The Self-Improving Memory System Inside Computer. Perplexity launched Brain—a continuously learning context graph for its Computer agent. Here's what it is and how it works. Perplexity post-trained GLM 5.2 for the Computer harness — research preview with an advisor tool that escalates to stronger models, ~half Opus cost on WANDR, hosted on US Nvidia B200s. This comparison breaks down what each tool actually does well, where each one falls short, and gives you a three-question framework to pick the right one for what you are building in 2026. **Brain** is a continuously learning context graph built into Perplexity's Computer agent—and unlike most "memory" features, it updates itself overnight and feeds automatically into every subsequent task. Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas announced it directly: *"Brain is a self-improving context-graph of all your sessions, connectors, and files. Brain updates itself overnight with fresh context proactively, and feeds itself to every task on Computer, allowing Computer to be stateful and self-improving."*. Brain is Perplexity's answer to that problem.","score":0.7971311,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/06/18/perplexity-launches-brain?amp=","title":"Perplexity Launches Brain, a Self-Improving Memory System That ...","content":"Home Editors Pick Agentic AI Perplexity Launches Brain, a Self-Improving Memory System That Builds a Context Graph... # Perplexity Launches Brain, a Self-Improving Memory System That Builds a Context Graph of an Agent’s Work and Learns Overnight. Most AI memory remembers the user. Today, Perplexity launched **Brain**, a self-improving memory system for its agent product, **Computer**. It remembers what the agent did. That reframes what memory in AI is for. ## **What is **Perplexity**‘s Brain**. Brain is a self-improving memory system. It builds a context graph of the work Computer performs. The **first** is what the memory is about. The **second** is what the memory is for. Its memory is about the agent’s work. | What it is about | The user | The agent’s work |. | What it is for | Feeling more engaged with the agent | Helping the agent get better at the job |. | What it produces | A profile of the user | A traceable context graph of the work |.","score":0.6877354,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=l06SDT34CbQ&vl=en","title":"Perplexity Launches ‘Computer’ | What Is It? AI firm unveils V4 as general-purpose digital worker—not a chatbot—handling research, reports, code, visuals & data generation autonomously. CEO: “Unifies every AI capability into a single system.” Built secretly for 2 months; available to Max subscribers, soon Pro. Shift toward AI autonomy amid race for complex task-handling.
